name: Identity

tasks.title: Implement Identity type and extraction functions
tasks.description: Implement Identity type and extraction functions
tasks.body:
Create internal/config/identity.go with:

- Identity struct: User string, Machine string, Namespace string (computed as User + "-" + Machine).
- IdentityFromConfig(cfg *Config) (*Identity, error): extracts identity from a loaded config. Returns error if cfg.Identity is nil or if User/Machine are empty. Sets Namespace to User + "-" + Machine.
- DetectIdentity() *Identity: reads username from os/user.Current() and hostname from os.Hostname(). Falls back to "unknown" for either if the system call fails. Sets Namespace from the detected values.
- (id *Identity) ProjectsDir(wolfcastleRoot string) string: returns filepath.Join(wolfcastleRoot, "system", "projects", id.Namespace).

This mirrors the logic currently in tree.ResolveNamespace and project.detectIdentity, but as a first-class type. The existing IdentityConfig struct in types.go remains as the config-tier representation; Identity is the domain type derived from it.
acceptance_criteria: Identity compiles with all spec signatures
IdentityFromConfig returns error for nil or incomplete identity

tasks.title: Test Identity type
tasks.body:
Create internal/config/identity_test.go with:

1. TestIdentityFromConfig_Valid: Provide a Config with Identity.User and Identity.Machine set. Verify the returned Identity has correct User, Machine, and Namespace (User + "-" + Machine).
2. TestIdentityFromConfig_NilIdentity: Pass a Config with nil Identity, verify error.
3. TestIdentityFromConfig_EmptyUser: Identity with empty User, verify error.
4. TestIdentityFromConfig_EmptyMachine: Identity with empty Machine, verify error.
5. TestDetectIdentity: Call DetectIdentity, verify it returns a non-nil Identity with non-empty User, Machine, and Namespace. (We can't assert exact values since they depend on the host, but we can verify the contract.)
6. TestIdentity_ProjectsDir: Create an Identity with known values, call ProjectsDir with a temp root, verify the returned path matches the expected structure (root/system/projects/namespace).

These are unit tests — no testutil.Environment needed since Identity has no filesystem dependencies beyond ProjectsDir path construction.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Identity is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify Identity is distinct from IdentityConfig in types.go — domain type vs config-tier type

breadcrumbs.text: Implemented Identity domain type in internal/config/identity.go with IdentityFromConfig, DetectIdentity, and ProjectsDir. Tests cover nil config, empty fields, valid extraction, OS detection, and path computation.
task-0002: Identity tests already exist from task-0001. All six test cases pass: config extraction (valid + three error paths), OS detection, and ProjectsDir path construction.

Audit complete: build, tests (including race detector), formatting, and vet all pass. Code reviewed for nil safety, error handling, edge cases, naming, and modularity. Created missing ADR for concrete-struct-with-constructor-validation decision and filled in Identity Domain Type Contract spec.

audit.result_summary: Implemented the Identity domain type in internal/config/identity.go, consolidating namespace resolution and OS detection into a single authoritative struct with two constructors (IdentityFromConfig for config extraction with validation, DetectIdentity for OS-based fallback detection) and a ProjectsDir method. Six tests cover all error paths, valid extraction, OS detection, and path construction. ADR documents the concrete-struct-with-constructor-validation design choice; spec documents the full contract including invariants and thread safety.
